questions.prompt: In DoDAF 2.02 Volume II, which DoDAF models are required for every architectural description, and what makes all the other viewpoint models optional? Name the principle this rests on.
must_include: AV-1 and AV-2 are the only required models
all other viewpoint models are optional / selected by decision-maker need
must_not_include: all DoDAF models must be produced for every architecture

questions.prompt: Describe the DM2 Resource Flow Triple. Why are direct performer-to-performer resource transfers not modeled in DoDAF?
must_include: resources flow because activities produce/consume them, not performers directly
a performer is affected only through the activities it performs
must_not_include: performers exchange resources directly without an intervening activity

questions.prompt: Name the eight DoDAF viewpoints and state how many DM2 data groups exist, distinguishing principle from supporting groups.
must_include: the eight viewpoints: All, Capability, Data and Information, Operational, Project, Services, Standards, Systems
eight principle data groups and three supporting data groups
must_not_include: Technical Standards Viewpoint as a ninth viewpoint

questions.prompt: A systems engineer wants to claim that a fielded system satisfies an operational need. Which DoDAF model provides the formal traceability for that claim, what two model sets does it link, and why can't this mapping introduce new elements?
must_include: SV-5a is the system-function-to-operational-activity traceability mapping
it links SV-4 system functions to OV-5b operational activities
SV-5a is a derived mapping that introduces no new elements
must_not_include: SV-5a may define new operational activities not present in OV-5b

questions.prompt: An architect modeling the Standards Viewpoint wants to attach a technical standard so that a system is compliant. In DoDAF, what does a standard bind to (and what does it NOT bind to), and where must technical standards for DoD IT come from?
must_include: standards bind to activities, not to performers directly
a performer is governed by a standard only through the activity it performs
technical standards for DoD IT must be drawn from the DISR

synonyms: DoD Information Technology Standards and Profile Registry

must_not_include: standards are related directly to performers in the Standards Viewpoint

questions.prompt: A capability portfolio manager needs to show which projects are delivering which capabilities. Which DoDAF Project Viewpoint model provides this, and what traceability chain does it complete?
must_include: PV-3 maps projects to the capabilities they deliver
it provides the program-elements to projects to capabilities traceability chain
must_not_include: PV-1 is the model that maps projects to capabilities

questions.prompt: My espresso machine keeps producing bitter shots. Should I adjust the grind size, the water temperature, or the tamping pressure to fix the extraction?

I'm building a DoDAF 2.02 architectural description and need to pick the right viewpoint models for a service-oriented system. Which SvcV models trace services to operational activities, and how does the DM2 treat a service relative to the performer-activity-resource triad?
